ops.text:
AMENDMENT (pre-spend, $1.96 probe only). STRUCTURAL SURPRISE from the screen lane: the pre-registered arms K∈{125,250,500,1000} as literal exemplar counts are context-INFEASIBLE — each exemplar carries draft_text() = the full context-window tail (~5k median chars), so K=125 = 325,358 tokens (measured); K=500≈1.37M and K=1000≈2.68M OVERFLOW sonnet's 1M ceiling. Only K∈{125,250} fit. The pre-reg cost model (~$13-22, ~50-132k prefix) was ~2-6x low. This is itself a FINDING: full-fidelity many-shot-as-watcher is context-bound at ~250 exemplars on this task because the watcher's exemplars are whole context tails, not short messages.

RULING (orchestrator, faithful-minimal): PRIMARY = Option A, K∈{125,250} full-fidelity — this remains genuine many-shot (125-250 exemplars) with full-fidelity demos that teach the real task (read context tail → predict steer), the minimal deviation. Kill rule adapts to a 2-point 125→250 trend: score the better K (250 if increasing) on the full 628; paired DeLong vs incumbent 0.9317 per the card rule. CONDITIONAL SECONDARY (only if the best full-fidelity watcher is competitive — within ~0.03 AUC of 0.9317 — AND 250>125 i.e. still climbing): add ONE truncated-exemplar higher-K arm (per-demo char cap so ~500 demos fit <1M) to disentangle count-vs-fidelity and probe the "many" regime the pre-reg intended; otherwise report the context ceiling and close. Report the corrected cost model + the exemplar-size ceiling as a headline finding. $25 cap stands. Everything else per the pre-reg (TRAIN pool already fully session-disjoint from the 628 — audit passed; DeLong; co-primary cost/latency; VERIFY; ledger). NOT re-registering arms wholesale (Option D) — the core question (can many-shot match FT?) is answered at the achievable ceiling.
